text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY -- FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Metastatic Breast Cancer -- Olaparib Cycle 9 (Approximately 6 Months on Therapy)

ATTENDING: [Attending Oncologist]
FELLOW/APP: [Fellow/APP]

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Ms. Okonkwo is a 39-year-old woman with metastatic triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), germline BRCA1 mutation, presenting for routine follow-up on olaparib (Lynparza) therapy, approximately 6 months since initiation.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Okonkwo is a 39-year-old nulliparous premenopausal woman of West African descent with a history of stage IIB (T2N1M0) grade 3 triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast diagnosed approximately 18 months prior. She received neoadjuvant pembrolizumab plus dose-dense AC (ddAC x4 cycles) followed by weekly Taxol x12 weeks with concurrent pembro, then underwent left lumpectomy with SLNB/ALND (ypT1c ypN0(i+), RCB-II, Miller-Payne grade 3). She completed adjuvant whole-breast radiation with regional nodal irradiation, adjuvant capecitabine x6 cycles (dose-reduced for grade 2 hand-foot syndrome), and completed a total of 17 cycles of adjuvant pembrolizumab. Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.3750G>A, p.E1250*) was confirmed at diagnosis.

Approximately 18 months after surgery she developed new right-sided rib pain, and restaging workup revealed metastatic disease with right 5th rib/posterior, T10 vertebral body, and right iliac wing osseous involvement, as well as two right hepatic lobe lesions (measuring 1.2 cm and 0.8 cm on initial staging CT). CT-guided biopsy of the right iliac wing confirmed metastatic triple-negative breast carcinoma; repeat IHC showed ER-, PR-, HER2 IHC 1+ (not amplified), GATA3+, CK7+. PD-L1 CPS on the metastatic specimen was 8 (below threshold for pembro rechallenge). NGS on the metastatic biopsy demonstrated the expected BRCA1 p.E1250* and TP53 p.R248W, with acquisition of PIK3CA p.H1047R and a new CDK4 amplification not seen on the primary tumor.

She received palliative radiation to the right 5th posterior rib (20 Gy in 5 fractions) with excellent pain response (5/10 down to 1/10). Olaparib 300 mg BID was initiated for first-line metastatic TNBC given germline BRCA1 mutation, along with zoledronic acid 4 mg IV every 4 weeks for bone-modifying therapy.

At her 3-month restaging (approximately cycle 4-5 of olaparib), CT showed the hepatic lesions had decreased -- the 1.2 cm lesion to 0.9 cm, the 0.8 cm to 0.6 cm -- and osseous sites were stable without new disease. She was continued on olaparib at full dose.

Today she presents for her 6-month on-therapy visit. She reports she is tolerating olaparib fairly well overall. She notes ongoing mild fatigue (grade 1, present most afternoons, does not prevent her from working part-time), and mild nausea intermittently, particularly in the morning shortly after taking her olaparib dose. She has been managing the nausea with ondansetron 4 mg PRN, which she uses 2-3 times per week. She reports taking olaparib consistently with food per instruction, which has helped somewhat. She denies vomiting. No diarrhea, constipation, or abdominal pain. She reports her rib pain is well-controlled, currently rated 0-1/10, using acetaminophen 1000 mg TID scheduled; she has not needed opioid breakthrough medication for approximately 6 weeks. She denies new bone pain. She denies dyspnea, cough, or chest pain. She reports stable mild tingling in her feet bilaterally, unchanged from prior (residual grade 1 neuropathy from prior paclitaxel), not worsening. She denies headache, visual changes, or new neurologic symptoms. No skin rash.

Importantly, she reports that her sister who was referred for BRCA1 testing returned a positive result for the same pathogenic variant. She is understandably supportive of her sister and somewhat emotionally burdened by this news. She has spoken with gynecologic oncology regarding risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (RRSO) for herself and reports she is ready to proceed; a date is being coordinated.

She remains on levothyroxine 50 mcg daily (pembrolizumab-associated hypothyroidism, well-controlled; last TSH normal).

She is currently not working full-time but is doing 3 days per week of part-time teaching, which she values highly for her sense of purpose and routine.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Positive: mild fatigue (grade 1), intermittent nausea (grade 1), bilateral foot paresthesias (stable grade 1, chronic). Mild dyspepsia with AM olaparib dose.
Negative: no vomiting, no significant diarrhea, no new bone pain, no chest pain, no dyspnea, no cough, no visual changes, no headache, no focal neurologic deficits, no skin rash, no oral mucositis, no new joint pains, no weight loss, no fevers, no night sweats, no hematuria, no rectal bleeding, no dysuria.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
General: Well-appearing woman, no acute distress. Wearing head covering (ongoing alopecia recovery, hair beginning to regrow). Affect appropriate, mildly anxious when discussing family history/sister's result.
Vitals: BP 114/72 mmHg, HR 74 bpm, RR 14, SpO2 98% on room air, weight 64.2 kg (down 0.8 kg from last visit, clinically stable), BMI 23.1 kg/m2.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oral mucosa moist, no ulcers or thrush. No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.
Chest/Breast: Left breast: expected post-lumpectomy contour change with mild fibrotic indentation at the upper outer quadrant. No new palpable mass. No skin thickening or erythema. Right breast: No palpable masses. Bilateral axillae: no palpable adenopathy.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. No peripheral edema.
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes or crackles.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly on palpation. No splenomegaly. No RUQ tenderness. Bowel sounds normoactive.
Musculoskeletal: No midline thoracic spine tenderness to palpation. Right posterior lower chest wall: mild residual tenderness at the 5th rib site, no crepitus, improved from prior. Right iliac wing: no point tenderness on deep palpation.
Neurologic: CN II-XII grossly intact. Decreased sensation to light touch in bilateral feet in a stocking distribution (stable from prior exam). Gait normal. No ataxia. Deep tendon reflexes 2+ bilateral lower extremities.
Skin: No palmar-plantar erythema (HFS resolved). No rash.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
CBC:
  WBC 5.1 x10^9/L (nl)
  Hemoglobin 10.2 g/dL (LOW; baseline 12.8 g/dL prior to olaparib)
  MCV 98 fL (macrocytic)
  Platelets 198 x10^9/L (nl)
  ANC 2.8 x10^9/L (nl)
  Reticulocyte count 1.1% (low-normal for degree of anemia)

Comprehensive metabolic panel:
  Sodium 139, Potassium 4.0, Creatinine 0.72 mg/dL (eGFR >60)
  Total bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL, Direct bilirubin 0.2 mg/dL
  ALT 28 U/L (nl), AST 22 U/L (nl), Alkaline phosphatase 88 U/L (mildly elevated but stable)
  Albumin 3.9 g/dL

Iron studies:
  Serum ferritin 68 ng/mL
  Serum iron 72 mcg/dL
  TIBC 310 mcg/dL
  Transferrin saturation 23% -- no evidence of iron-deficiency anemia; consistent with PARP inhibitor-related macrocytic anemia
  Vitamin B12 and folate: within normal limits (checked to exclude nutritional deficiency contributing to macrocytosis)

Thyroid function:
  TSH 2.4 mIU/L (normal; well-controlled on levothyroxine 50 mcg)

Lipase: 42 U/L (nl)

---

IMAGING
No new imaging obtained at today's visit. Most recent restaging CT (approximately 3 months ago, cycle 4-5 of olaparib) showed:
  - Right hepatic lobe lesions: 1.2 cm lesion decreased to 0.9 cm; 0.8 cm lesion decreased to 0.6 cm (ongoing response)
  - Osseous sites (T10, right iliac wing): stable, no interval progression
  - No new pulmonary or hepatic lesions
  - Right 5th rib: post-radiation changes, structurally intact
Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast is planned for approximately 3 months from today (approximately 9 months on olaparib).

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. METASTATIC TRIPLE-NEGATIVE BREAST CANCER, germline BRCA1 p.E1250* (c.3750G>A), with bone (T10, right 5th rib, right iliac wing) and hepatic (right lobe x2) involvement -- currently on olaparib (Lynparza) 300 mg BID, cycle 9, approximately 6 months on therapy.
   - Disease status: partial response ongoing by prior restaging; no new clinical or symptomatic evidence of progression today
   - Continue olaparib 300 mg BID -- dose is appropriate; no dose reduction indicated for current toxicity profile
   - Continue zoledronic acid 4 mg IV every 4 weeks (bone-modifying agent for skeletal metastases); next dose administered today in infusion center. Confirm patient is continuing calcium 1200 mg/day and vitamin D 800 IU/day supplementation. Dental health monitoring maintained.
   - Restaging CT CAP with contrast scheduled in approximately 3 months for ongoing response assessment

2. OLAPARIB-RELATED ANEMIA (grade 1, hemoglobin 10.2 g/dL, macrocytic pattern, MCV 98):
   - Anemia is consistent with PARP inhibitor class effect -- myelosuppression causing impaired DNA repair in hematopoietic progenitors leading to macrocytosis and mild anemia
   - No iron deficiency identified; B12/folate WNL; not attributable to bleeding or hemolysis
   - Grade 1 (Hgb 10.0-12.0 g/dL in women) -- no dose reduction required at this threshold per protocol
   - Patient is asymptomatic from an anemia standpoint (fatigue is grade 1 and multifactorial)
   - Repeat CBC at next visit in 6 weeks; if Hgb drops below 9.0 g/dL or patient becomes symptomatic, will reassess dose modification
   - Patient educated that this pattern is expected and to report any worsening dyspnea on exertion, palpitations, or significant dizziness

3. GRADE 1 NAUSEA ON OLAPARIB:
   - Patient is taking olaparib with food -- reinforce this consistently; splitting doses (morning with breakfast, evening with dinner) may be beneficial
   - Ondansetron 4 mg PRN at time of dosing is appropriate; encourage using it prophylactically before AM dose if nausea is predictable
   - No dose reduction warranted for grade 1 nausea

4. GRADE 1 FATIGUE:
   - Multifactorial in context of metastatic disease, mild anemia, ongoing systemic therapy
   - Encouraged light aerobic activity as tolerated (walking program); referred to integrated oncology for fatigue management support
   - Discussed energy conservation strategies given part-time work schedule

5. RESIDUAL GRADE 1 PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY (bilateral feet, stable):
   - Unchanged from prior; no worsening -- reassuring given discontinuation of paclitaxel many months ago
   - Continuing duloxetine 60 mg daily for symptomatic management
   - Foot care precautions reinforced

6. HYPOTHYROIDISM (pembrolizumab-associated, adjuvant therapy):
   - Well-controlled on levothyroxine 50 mcg daily; TSH 2.4 today -- no dose adjustment needed
   - Continue current regimen

7. GERMLINE BRCA1 MUTATION -- RISK-REDUCING SURGERY:
   - Patient is interested in proceeding with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (RRSO) given confirmed germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant and ovarian cancer risk (maternal aunt with ovarian ca at 52, maternal grandmother with breast ca)
   - Coordination underway with gynecologic oncology; patient was advised that RRSO in a premenopausal BRCA1 carrier is typically recommended between ages 35-40
   - Discussed that olaparib can be continued through surgical planning; brief perioperative hold (~1 week) to be coordinated with gynecology
   - Discussed potential for surgical menopause and management options in the context of hormone-receptor-negative disease (systemic HRT is generally avoided; non-hormonal options for vasomotor symptoms: venlafaxine, gabapentin, etc.)

8. PSYCHOSOCIAL:
   - Patient's sister has tested positive for BRCA1 germline mutation; patient expressing emotional burden and worry for family members
   - Social work referral placed; ongoing support offered
   - Patient is managing remarkably well emotionally; continues to find meaning in her work as a teacher
   - Patient and family encouraged to remain engaged with genetic counseling resources

FOLLOW-UP: In 6 weeks for CBC recheck and symptom review. Full restaging CT in approximately 3 months. Zoledronic acid infusion today as scheduled. Gynecologic oncology RRSO appointment to be confirmed.

Time spent: 45 minutes, greater than 50% in counseling and coordination of care.

[Electronically signed by Attending Oncologist]
